{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Petrov's Defence: Italian Variation| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd| | | | | |nd| | | | | | |pd| | | | | |bl| |pl| | | | | | | | |nl| | |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|rl|nl|bl|ql|kl| | |rl|| }} == Petrov's Defence: Italian Variation == ===3. Bc4=== This move is usually played by beginners, who are used to the Italian Game, however this does leave the e4 pawn hanging. Black can take the undefended e4 pawn with [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nf6/3. Bc4/3...Nxe4|3...Nxe4]], but could also just transpose to the Two Knights Defence with [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bc4/3...Nf6|3...Nc6]]. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. e4 e5 2. Bc4 Nf6 3.Nf3''' or '''1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nf6 3.Bc4'''
